verses.text: Isaiah's vision opens with God's complaint: the ox knows its owner, but Israel does not know, His own children rebelling. Even beasts honour their master. It is a deep shame when God's people show less sense of Him than dumb animals.

Isaiah's vision opens with God's complaint: the ox knows its owner, but Israel does not know, His own children rebelling. Even beasts honour their master. It is a deep shame when God's people show less sense of Him than dumb animals.

Isaiah's vision opens with God's complaint: the ox knows its owner, but Israel does not know, His own children rebelling. Even beasts honour their master. It is a deep shame when God's people show less sense of Him than dumb animals.
A sinful nation, laden with iniquity, is sick from head to foot, 'no soundness... but wounds and bruises.' Sin has diseased the whole body. Israel's corruption is total, beyond any human cure.
A sinful nation, laden with iniquity, is sick from head to foot, 'no soundness... but wounds and bruises.' Sin has diseased the whole body. Israel's corruption is total, beyond any human cure.
A sinful nation, laden with iniquity, is sick from head to foot, 'no soundness... but wounds and bruises.' Sin has diseased the whole body. Israel's corruption is total, beyond any human cure.

The land lies desolate, cities burned, and only because the LORD left a small remnant are they not as Sodom. Judgment has already fallen hard. God's mercy in sparing a remnant is all that stands between them and utter ruin.

The land lies desolate, cities burned, and only because the LORD left a small remnant are they not as Sodom. Judgment has already fallen hard. God's mercy in sparing a remnant is all that stands between them and utter ruin.

The land lies desolate, cities burned, and only because the LORD left a small remnant are they not as Sodom. Judgment has already fallen hard. God's mercy in sparing a remnant is all that stands between them and utter ruin.

God calls them rulers of Sodom and asks what their many sacrifices are to Him, weary of their offerings. Ritual without obedience disgusts Him. God takes no pleasure in worship divorced from a changed life.

God calls them rulers of Sodom and asks what their many sacrifices are to Him, weary of their offerings. Ritual without obedience disgusts Him. God takes no pleasure in worship divorced from a changed life.

God calls them rulers of Sodom and asks what their many sacrifices are to Him, weary of their offerings. Ritual without obedience disgusts Him. God takes no pleasure in worship divorced from a changed life.

He hates their feasts and hides His eyes from their outspread hands, 'your hands are full of blood.' Bloody hands cannot offer acceptable prayer. Worship from an unrepentant heart only adds to the offence.

He hates their feasts and hides His eyes from their outspread hands, 'your hands are full of blood.' Bloody hands cannot offer acceptable prayer. Worship from an unrepentant heart only adds to the offence.

He hates their feasts and hides His eyes from their outspread hands, 'your hands are full of blood.' Bloody hands cannot offer acceptable prayer. Worship from an unrepentant heart only adds to the offence.
'Wash you, make you clean'; cease from evil, learn to do good, defend the oppressed, the orphan, the widow. God calls for real reform. True religion turns from sin and shows mercy to the helpless.
'Wash you, make you clean'; cease from evil, learn to do good, defend the oppressed, the orphan, the widow. God calls for real reform. True religion turns from sin and shows mercy to the helpless.

'Come now, and let us reason together... though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.' God offers full pardon. The deepest stain of sin can be made spotless by the grace He freely holds out.

'Come now, and let us reason together... though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.' God offers full pardon. The deepest stain of sin can be made spotless by the grace He freely holds out.

'Come now, and let us reason together... though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.' God offers full pardon. The deepest stain of sin can be made spotless by the grace He freely holds out.

The faithful city has become a harlot, her silver dross, her princes companions of thieves who love bribes and ignore the fatherless. Corruption has spread to the top. When leaders are bought, justice for the weak dies.

The faithful city has become a harlot, her silver dross, her princes companions of thieves who love bribes and ignore the fatherless. Corruption has spread to the top. When leaders are bought, justice for the weak dies.

The faithful city has become a harlot, her silver dross, her princes companions of thieves who love bribes and ignore the fatherless. Corruption has spread to the top. When leaders are bought, justice for the weak dies.
The Lord will turn His hand to purge away the dross and restore upright judges 'as at the first.' Judgment is also refining. God afflicts to purify, that the city may be righteous again.

'Zion shall be redeemed with judgment,' but transgressors shall be destroyed and ashamed of the idols they desired. Two ends await. The penitent are redeemed; those who cling to their idols perish with them.
